_description: Mars GCM Weather Model retroactive enrichment - adds atmospheric physics hazards to existing frames

_physics_added.seasonal_pressure_variation: CO2 sublimation/deposition cycle causes 610-1155 Pa pressure swings affecting ISRU efficiency
_physics_added.enhanced_dust_storm_physics: Radiative-dynamic feedback creates more realistic dust storm effects with orbital timing
_physics_added.orbital_solar_variation: 45% solar flux variation from Mars' 0.0934 eccentricity affects power and thermal systems
_physics_added.uv_radiation_degradation: UV-C reaches Mars surface causing cumulative material degradation of polymers/plastics
_physics_added.atmospheric_density_effects: Variable atmospheric density affects dust transport and convective cooling
_physics_added.physics_integration: Seasonal pressure directly affects Sabatier reactor efficiency via CO2 availability

_enrichment_strategy: Retroactive Mars GCM hazards added to existing frames to create realistic atmospheric physics progression. Seasonal pressure and solar flux variations follow actual Mars orbital mechanics. UV degradation accumulates realistically causing progressive system failures. Dust storm effects enhanced with real radiative-dynamic feedback physics.
